verdict: AI displacement risk: High, but with opportunities for adjacent roles

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 2, 3, and 8 are routine cognitive tasks that can be automated by LLMs

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: Task 4 requires creative judgment, but this is not a dominant aspect of the job

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory Moat
reasoning_steps.body: The regulatory moat is moderate due to licensure requirements and statutory liability

reasoning_steps.title: Embodied Work
reasoning_steps.body: Task 9 requires monitoring use of data files, but this can be done remotely

reasoning_steps.title: Stakes of Error
reasoning_steps.body: The stakes of error are high due to the potential for system breaches and viruses

evidence_ledger.contribution: O*NET task list provided routine cognitive tasks
Wikipedia summary provided context on information security

adjacent_careers.name: Information Security Analyst
adjacent_careers.reasoning: This role is highly similar to Cybersecurity Analyst, with some differences in scope and emphasis

adjacent_careers.name: Penetration Tester
adjacent_careers.reasoning: This role requires some overlap with Cybersecurity Analyst, but has a different focus on testing systems

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered security tool detects vulnerabilities in real-time
recent_developments.impact: This development raises concerns about the potential for AI to automate some aspects of cybersecurity analysis

recent_developments.headline: AI coding assistants see broad enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Major firms report 25–30% of new code is now AI-generated and reviewed by engineers.

quotes.quote: Software engineering will be very different by the end of 2025. Most code will be written by AI, with human engineers reviewing and directing.
quotes.speaker: Dario Amodei
quotes.title: CEO, Anthropic

quotes.quote: AI is going to be the biggest technological shift of our lifetimes. Bigger than mobile, bigger than the web. Inside Microsoft, more than 30 percent of the code in some projects is now written by AI.
quotes.speaker: Satya Nadella
quotes.title: CEO, Microsoft

quotes.quote: More than a quarter of new code at Google is generated by AI, then reviewed and accepted by engineers.
quotes.speaker: Sundar Pichai
quotes.title: CEO, Google / Alphabet
